commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a gateway-backed WhatsApp pairing approval notification path via a new `delivery: "outbound-message"` `createChatChannelPlugin` pairing mode, documents it, and adds focused pairing tests.

PR surface: Source +104, Tests +167, Docs +13. Total +284 across 6 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level. Current main exposes `openclaw pairing approve --notify`, but `notifyPairingApproved` returns when the adapter lacks `notifyApproval`, and the WhatsApp adapter currently lacks that hook.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human review is needed for plugin SDK contract acceptance and contributor live proof; there is no narrow mechanical repair to queue from this review.

target.title: feat(whatsapp): notify sender after pairing approval
target.body:
Restored replacement for #94386 after the fork/head repository was recreated.

## Summary

- Add WhatsApp approval notifications to the existing channel pairing `--notify` path by routing approval delivery through the shared outbound `sendMessage` gateway path.
- Keep the public pairing adapter surface closed; the WhatsApp path uses the `createChatChannelPlugin` text pairing `delivery: "outbound-message"` mode instead of loose adapter fields.
- Document the outbound-message pairing delivery mode in the channel plugin SDK docs.
- Add and fix focused WhatsApp pairing coverage for notification wiring and normalization.

Fixes #56619.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/channels/plugins/pairing.test.ts src/channels/plugins/pairing-adapters.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/channel.pairing.test.ts src/cli/pairing-cli.test.ts --reporter=dot`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.tooling.config.ts test/scripts/plugin-prerelease-test-plan.test.ts --reporter=dot`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/whatsapp/src/channel.pairing.test.ts --reporter=dot`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/whatsapp/src/channel.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/channel.pairing.test.ts src/channels/plugins/pairing.types.ts src/channels/plugins/pairing-adapters.ts src/channels/plugins/pairing-adapters.test.ts src/channels/plugins/pairing.ts src/plugin-sdk/core.ts src/channels/plugins/pairing.test.ts`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/whatsapp/src/channel.pairing.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Approving a WhatsApp pairing request with notification enabled now sends the shared pairing-approved message back through the OpenClaw outbound send path, so WhatsApp delivery is handled by the running gateway instead of a short-lived local helper.

Real environment tested: Local full OpenClaw checkout on PR branch `codex/whatsapp-pairing-approval-notify-56619-20260618`, exercising the WhatsApp pairing adapter, plugin SDK text pairing builder, pairing CLI channel wiring, and shared pairing adapter helpers through the repository Vitest harness after the patch.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/channels/plugins/pairing.test.ts src/channels/plugins/pairing-adapters.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/channel.pairing.test.ts src/cli/pairing-cli.test.ts --reporter=dot
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.tooling.config.ts test/scripts/plugin-prerelease-test-plan.test.ts --reporter=dot
node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/whatsapp/src/channel.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/channel.pairing.test.ts src/channels/plugins/pairing.types.ts src/channels/plugins/pairing-adapters.ts src/channels/plugins/pairing-adapters.test.ts src/channels/plugins/pairing.ts src/plugin-sdk/core.ts src/channels/plugins/pairing.test.ts
git diff --check
```

Evidence after fix: Terminal capture from the focused pairing run showed the gateway-backed notification wiring and adapter behavior passing:

```text
[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.cli.config.ts
[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.extension-whatsapp.config.ts
[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.channels.config.ts

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests       13 passed (13)

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests       2 passed (2)

Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests       4 passed (4)

[test] passed 3 Vitest shards
```

Observed result after fix: The plugin SDK pairing test verifies `delivery: "outbound-message"` creates a `notifyApproval` closure that calls the shared outbound `sendMessage` with the approved target id, shared approval message, channel id, account id, and config. The WhatsApp test verifies the plugin exposes `notifyApproval` without loose pairing adapter fields, and the normalization test verifies WhatsApp-prefixed phone entries still normalize correctly. CLI and shared adapter tests remain green.

What was not tested: A live WhatsApp transport session was not started in this environment; the focused plugin pairing tests exercise the changed approval notification path without external WhatsApp credentials.
